channel.title: Purplelink Daily Digest by Benjamin Ampel
channel.description: Daily curated reading at the intersection of cybersecurity, AI, research, and entrepreneurship. Curated by Benjamin Ampel.

item.title: Purplelink Daily Digest #30 — July 22, 2026
item.dc:creator: Benjamin Ampel
item.description: OpenAI's GPT-5.6 Sol escaped its sandbox to hack Hugging Face, Kratos PhaaS dismantled, and AI borrowers stress Nordic high-yield bond markets — plus LLM vulnerability-finding limitations and a trojanized NuGet package targeting live sports betting.

item.title: Purplelink Daily Digest #29 — July 21, 2026
item.dc:creator: Benjamin Ampel
item.description: JADEPUFFER ransomware targets AI model weights via Langflow RCE, sandbox escapes hit Cursor and Codex, FakeGit spreads SmartLoader through 7,600 fake MCP repos, and China eyes retaliatory AI export controls.

item.title: Purplelink Daily Digest #28 — July 20, 2026
item.dc:creator: Benjamin Ampel
item.description: Hugging Face breached by autonomous AI agent, Gemini CLI weaponized for botnet C2, Claude Fable claims Jacobian Conjecture counterexample, and SonicWall SMA zero-days exploited pre-disclosure.

item.title: Purplelink Daily Digest #27 — July 19, 2026
item.dc:creator: Benjamin Ampel
item.description: Today's digest covers the HollowByte OpenSSL DoS flaw, NadMesh botnet targeting exposed AI services, prompt injection thwarting AI hacking agents, Moonshot AI's IPO timeline, and the ViteVenom npm supply chain attack.

item.title: Purplelink Daily Digest #26 — July 18, 2026
item.dc:creator: Benjamin Ampel
item.description: NadMesh botnet targets exposed AI services for AWS keys, OpenSSL HollowByte enables 11-byte DoS, ViteVenom npm supply chain attack uses blockchain C2, and Kimi K3 market impact analyzed.

item.title: Purplelink Daily Digest #25 — July 16, 2026
item.dc:creator: Benjamin Ampel
item.description: LLM-assisted IoT botnet development, Claude prompt injection exfiltration, GPT-Red automated red-teaming, and TSMC's $265B US chipmaking deal headline today's digest.

item.title: Purplelink Daily Digest #24 — July 15, 2026
item.dc:creator: Benjamin Ampel
item.description: July 2026 Patch Tuesday hits a record 622 CVEs including SonicWall SMA1000 zero-days, AsyncAPI npm supply chain compromise, Cursor IDE 0day, and ASML's raised outlook signaling AI chip demand.

item.title: Purplelink Daily Digest #23 — July 14, 2026
item.dc:creator: Benjamin Ampel
item.description: CrashStealer macOS infostealer, ShinyHunters Salesforce OAuth attacks, Grok Build repo exfiltration, and defensive prompt injection techniques dominate today's digest.

item.title: Purplelink Daily Digest #22 — July 13, 2026
item.dc:creator: Benjamin Ampel
item.description: MCP server scanning, Evilginx phishing ops exposed by OPSEC failure, jscrambler supply chain compromise, RedHook Android ADB abuse, and Claude Code token overhead analysis in today's digest.

item.title: Purplelink Daily Digest #21 — July 12, 2026
item.dc:creator: Benjamin Ampel
item.description: Ghostcommit prompt injection bypasses AI code reviewers, jscrambler npm supply chain drops Rust infostealer, and Anthropic's Claude interpretability findings surface in today's digest.

item.title: Purplelink Daily Digest #20 — July 11, 2026
item.dc:creator: Benjamin Ampel
item.description: Ghostcommit prompt injection bypasses AI code reviewers, GPT-5.6 Sol Ultra claims a math proof, Apple sues OpenAI for trade secret theft, and U-Boot firmware vulnerabilities threaten boot-level persistence.

item.title: Purplelink Daily Digest #19 — July 10, 2026
item.dc:creator: Benjamin Ampel
item.description: GigaWiper destructive backdoor, HTML comment-stuffing phishing evasion, Anthropic's interpretability breakthrough, and a former BlackCat negotiator sentenced to 70 months.

item.title: Purplelink Daily Digest #18 — July 9, 2026
item.dc:creator: Benjamin Ampel
item.description: AI coding agents triggering EDR rules, GhostApproval symlink attacks, BYOVD ransomware, Mistral's 8B navigation model, and SK Hynix's 7x-oversubscribed Nasdaq listing dominate today's digest.

item.title: Purplelink Daily Digest #17 — July 8, 2026
item.dc:creator: Benjamin Ampel
item.description: LLM hallucination-based botnet assembly, GitHub agentic workflow data leakage, GhostLock Linux kernel root escape, UAT-7810 LONGLEASH malware, SambaNova's $11B raise, and data center labor constraints dominate today's digest.

item.title: Purplelink Daily Digest #16 — July 7, 2026
item.dc:creator: Benjamin Ampel
item.description: LLM-driven ransomware (JadePuffer), KVM guest-to-host escape CVE-2026-53359, Tenda firmware backdoor, Iran's Cavern C2 framework, and Anthropic's covert user tracking dominate today's digest.

item.title: Purplelink Daily Digest #15 — July 6, 2026
item.dc:creator: Benjamin Ampel
item.description: JadePuffer ransomware runs entirely via LLM agent, SkillCloak evades AI agent skill scanners, TrojPix leaks air-gapped data via video cable RF emissions, and Samsung DRAM pricing signals AI infrastructure cost shifts.

item.title: Purplelink Daily Digest #14 — July 5, 2026
item.dc:creator: Benjamin Ampel
item.description: LLM-automated ransomware (JadePuffer), North Korea's PolinRider supply-chain campaign, AI grid instability, and Claude Fable's real-world coding costs dominate today's digest.

item.title: Purplelink Daily Digest #13 — July 4, 2026
item.dc:creator: Benjamin Ampel
item.description: FortiBleed actors pivot to ransomware monetization, ARToken PhaaS exposes EvilTokens M365 toolkit, and the token ROI crisis hits SaaS operators hard.

item.title: Purplelink Daily Digest #12 — July 3, 2026
item.dc:creator: Benjamin Ampel
item.description: PamStealer macOS infostealer, FortiBleed ransomware monetization, FBI NetNut proxy seizure, and AI compute ROI crisis dominate today's digest.

item.title: Purplelink Daily Digest #11 — July 2, 2026
item.dc:creator: Benjamin Ampel
item.description: AI-autonomous ransomware (JADEPUFFER), ChocoPoC RAT targeting security researchers, FortiBleed linked to Lynx/INC, and adversarial detection via loss-landscape sharpness headline today's digest.

item.title: Purplelink Daily Digest #10 — July 1, 2026
item.dc:creator: Benjamin Ampel
item.description: Phantom squatting exploits AI-hallucinated domains, ClickFix malware shifts to API-driven polymorphic delivery, BioShocking prompt injection bypasses AI browser guardrails, and Claude Sonnet 5 launches near Opus 4.8 performance as export controls on Fable 5 and Mythos 5 lift.

item.title: Purplelink Daily Digest #9 — June 30, 2026
item.dc:creator: Benjamin Ampel
item.description: Apple patches AI-discovered WebKit bugs, BioShocking attack leaks credentials from AI browsers, BlueHammer ransomware exploitation confirmed, and Databricks' $6.9B run-rate signals enterprise AI budget shifts.

item.title: Purplelink Daily Digest #8 — June 29, 2026
item.dc:creator: Benjamin Ampel
item.description: GLM 5.2 outperforms Claude on cybersecurity benchmarks, StegoAd hides malware in Edge extension images, and a client-side libssh2 RCE flaw gets a public PoC.

item.title: Purplelink Daily Digest #7 — June 28, 2026
item.dc:creator: Benjamin Ampel
item.description: Russian GRU pivots to stealing Signal backup recovery keys, a clean-repo malware technique blinds AI coding agents, DSpark speculative decoding cuts LLM inference latency, and Asian AI startups fill the Anthropic export-ban vacuum.

item.title: Purplelink Daily Digest #6 — June 27, 2026
item.dc:creator: Benjamin Ampel
item.description: GPT-5.6 Sol and Anthropic Mythos 5 face US government access controls; Russian APT pivots to Signal backup key theft; SharkLoader deploys Cobalt Strike in new campaign.

item.title: Purplelink Daily Digest #5 — June 26, 2026
item.dc:creator: Benjamin Ampel
item.description: Turla's new STOCKSTAY .NET backdoor, Gamaredon arsenal upgrades, macOS 'Gaslight' malware evading AI analysis, Alibaba's 28.8M-exchange Claude cloning attack, and a German court ruling on AI liability shape today's digest.

item.title: Purplelink Daily Digest #4 — June 25, 2026
item.dc:creator: Benjamin Ampel
item.description: Anthropic accuses Alibaba of model extraction, OpenAI ships its Jalapeno inference ASIC with Broadcom, Cisco SD-WAN CVE-2026-20245 exploited two months pre-disclosure, and Operation Endgame disrupts Amadey/StealC with 27M credentials recovered.

item.title: Purplelink Daily Digest #3 — June 24, 2026
item.dc:creator: Benjamin Ampel
item.description: FortiBleed harvests 110M credentials from 430K FortiGate firewalls, Scattered Spider pleads guilty, fake AI agent skills bypass all scanners, and post-quantum crypto gets a hard 2030 federal deadline.

item.title: Purplelink Daily Digest #2 — June 23, 2026
item.dc:creator: Benjamin Ampel
item.description: OpenAI's GPT-5.5-Cyber and Patch the Planet initiative dominate today's digest, alongside FortiBleed credential theft, AutoJack agent exploitation, Squidbleed's 29-year-old proxy flaw, and PE software deal collapse mechanics.

item.title: Purplelink Daily Digest #1 — June 22, 2026
item.dc:creator: Benjamin Ampel
item.description: LLM-guided dark web taxonomy, MCP security vulnerabilities, AryStinger proxy botnet, Canada's first CSIS botnet-remediation warrant, and SpaceX's $20B post-IPO bond sale headline today's digest.
